Title: The potato developer (D) locus encodes an R2R3 MYB transcription factor that regulates expression of multiple anthocyanin structural genes in tuber skin

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones to form dihydroflavonols in the anthocyanin/flavonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for anthocyanin production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides red/purple pigmentation in potato tuber skin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is associated with colored tuber skin; weakly expressed in white-skinned clones, indicating partial positive association with anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in tuber periderm of purple/red parent and colored progeny; weakly expressed in white parent and white progeny.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR expression analysis in segregating population tuber periderm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young tubers (1–3 cm diameter) from plants not yet senesced
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. f3h was expressed in colored tuber skin. 2. f3h expression was only weak in white-skinned clones. 3. Its expression pattern supports coordinated activation of anthocyanin structural genes by the D locus.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a structural gene target assayed in Results; not itself the primary mapped causal locus.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides red/purple pigmentation in potato tuber skin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expressed in colored tuber skin but not in white-skinned clones, consistent with positive contribution to anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in tuber periderm of colored parent and colored progeny; not detected in white parent or white progeny.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR expression analysis in segregating population tuber periderm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young tubers (1–3 cm diameter) from plants not yet senesced
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dfr expression strictly tracked colored tuber skin. 2. dfr was absent from white-skinned progeny. 3. The result supports coordinated control of anthocyanin structural genes by the D locus.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assayed as a downstream structural gene responsive to D/Stan2 activity.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation branch leading to delphinidin/petunidin-type anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid intermediates at the 3' and 5' positions, enabling synthesis of purple delphinidin/petunidin-type anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward purple anthocyanin branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to purple pigmentation in potato tuber skin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expressed in colored tuber skin and absent in white-skinned clones, consistent with positive contribution to anthocyanin accumulation and purple branch formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in tuber periderm of colored parent and colored progeny; not detected in white parent or white progeny.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR expression analysis in segregating population tuber periderm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young tubers (1–3 cm diameter) from plants not yet senesced
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. f3'5'h expression was present in colored tuber skin and absent in white tuber skin. 2. Its expression pattern indicates coordinated activation with other anthocyanin genes by D. 3. It likely supports the purple anthocyanin branch in pigmented skin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A functional allele of f3'5'h had been used in prior work cited for pigment profile comparison, but that prior transgene result is background context here.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/coordinately promotes expression of multiple anthocyanin structural genes in tuber skin.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major switch/required regulator for anthocyanin accumulation in potato tuber skin; corresponds to the D locus.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides red and purple pigmentation in tuber skin and other tissues when ectopically expressed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in tuber skin and, under constitutive overexpression, induces pigment accumulation in foliage, flowers, tuber skin, and tuber flesh.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W5281.2; 07506-01; Y83-5; Désirée; Bintje; diploid dd Rr pp and dd Rr Pp progeny
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in tuber skin of red/purple clones and colored progeny; not expressed in white-skinned clones; not expressed in tuber flesh of tested cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Doubled CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) with tobacco etch virus translational enhancer for transgenic overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA library screening; sequence analysis; cosegregation with pigmented skin in 136 F1 progeny; allele-specific RT-PCR/restriction assay showing only Stan2777 expression; transgenic overexpression/complementation in Désirée, Bintje, and white diploid clones; pigment/TLC analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young tubers (1–3 cm diameter) from plants not yet senesced; constitutive transgene expression tested in multiple tissues
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Not experimentally shown in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive expression caused ectopic pigmentation in multiple tissues; complete flesh pigmentation was not achieved, suggesting background-dependent or promoter-limited effects.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Functional Stan2 alleles are useful markers for breeding pigmented potato skin; overexpression can enhance anthocyanin content; the Stan2816-linked marker is useful for tracking red-skin alleles and potentially dosage-based color intensity selection.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Stan2 encodes an R2R3 MYB transcription factor and is the best-supported candidate for the potato D locus. 2. Stan2 expression is specific to colored tuber skin and only the Stan2777 allele was expressed in colored progeny. 3. Constitutive expression of Stan2777 induced anthocyanin accumulation in multiple tissues and complemented white-skinned genotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Stan2777 cosegregated perfectly with colored skin in 136 progeny. Stan2816 was common in 14/15 red-skinned white-fleshed tetraploid cultivars and absent from all white-skinned clones tested. Functional vs nonfunctional D alleles appear to differ mainly at the transcriptional level, not coding sequence disruption.
